speaker: MARTIUS
text: Yonder comes news. A wager they have met.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: My horse to yours, no.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: ’Tis done.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Agreed.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Say, has our general met the enemy?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: They lie in view but have not spoke as yet.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: So the good horse is mine.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: I’ll buy him of you.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: No, I’ll nor sell nor give him. Lend you him I will

speaker: LARTIUS
text: For half a hundred years.—Summon the town.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: How far off lie these armies?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Within this mile and half.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Then shall we hear their ’larum and they ours.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Now, Mars, I prithee, make us quick in work,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That we with smoking swords may march from hence

speaker: MARTIUS
text: To help our fielded friends!—Come, blow thy blast.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Tullus Aufidius, is he within your walls?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: No, nor a man that fears you less than he:

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: That’s lesser than a little.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Hark, our drums

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Are bringing forth our youth. We’ll break our walls

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Rather than they shall pound us up. Our gates,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Which yet seem shut, we have but pinned with rushes.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: They’ll open of themselves.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Hark you, far off!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: There is Aufidius. List what work he makes

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SENATOR
text: Amongst your cloven army.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: O, they are at it!

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Their noise be our instruction.—Ladders, ho!

speaker: MARTIUS
text: They fear us not but issue forth their city.—

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Now put your shields before your hearts, and fight

speaker: MARTIUS
text: With hearts more proof than shields.—Advance, brave Titus.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: They do disdain us much beyond our thoughts,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Which makes me sweat with wrath.—Come on, my fellows!

speaker: MARTIUS
text: He that retires, I’ll take him for a Volsce,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And he shall feel mine edge.

speaker: MARTIUS
text: All the contagion of the south light on you,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: You shames of Rome! You herd of—Boils and plagues

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Plaster you o’er, that you may be abhorred

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Farther than seen, and one infect another

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Against the wind a mile! You souls of geese,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: That bear the shapes of men, how have you run

speaker: MARTIUS
text: From slaves that apes would beat! Pluto and hell!

speaker: MARTIUS
text: All hurt behind. Backs red, and faces pale

speaker: MARTIUS
text: With flight and agued fear! Mend, and charge home,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Or, by the fires of heaven, I’ll leave the foe

speaker: MARTIUS
text: And make my wars on you. Look to ’t. Come on!

speaker: MARTIUS
text: If you’ll stand fast, we’ll beat them to their wives,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: As they us to our trenches. Follow ’s!

speaker: MARTIUS
text: So, now the gates are ope. Now prove good seconds!

speaker: MARTIUS
text: ’Tis for the followers fortune widens them,

speaker: MARTIUS
text: Not for the fliers. Mark me, and do the like.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SOLDIER
text: Foolhardiness, not I.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SOLDIER
text: Nor I.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SOLDIER
text: See they have shut him in.

speaker: ALL
text: To th’ pot, I warrant him.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: What is become of Martius?

speaker: ALL
text: Slain, sir, doubtless.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SOLDIER
text: Following the fliers at the very heels,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SOLDIER
text: With them he enters, who upon the sudden

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SOLDIER
text: Clapped to their gates. He is himself alone,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SOLDIER
text: To answer all the city.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: O, noble fellow,

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Who sensibly outdares his senseless sword,

speaker: LARTIUS
text: And when it bows, stand’st up! Thou art left, Martius.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: A carbuncle entire, as big as thou art,

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Were not so rich a jewel. Thou wast a soldier

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Even to Cato’s wish, not fierce and terrible

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Only in strokes, but with thy grim looks and

speaker: LARTIUS
text: The thunderlike percussion of thy sounds

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Thou mad’st thine enemies shake, as if the world

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Were feverous and did tremble.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SOLDIER
text: Look, sir.

speaker: LARTIUS
text: O, ’tis Martius!

speaker: LARTIUS
text: Let’s fetch him off or make remain alike.
